title: Share of GDP per capita spent by the government on each primary school student vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: GDP per capita data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Share of GDP per capita spent by the government on each primary school student vs. GDP per capita

GDP per capita data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This scatter plot displays 2018 data comparing government expenditure per primary school student, expressed as a percentage of GDP per capita, against the GDP per capita itself. The vertical axis ranges from 0% to over 40%, while the horizontal axis represents GDP per capita in international dollars at 2021 prices, plotted on a logarithmic scale from $2,000 to $100,000. Points are color-coded by region, including North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. Serbia sits at the highest point on the chart, exceeding 40% of GDP per capita. Djibouti is positioned near 38%, followed by Albania and Ukraine in the 30% to 35% range. Cyprus and South Korea appear near the 30% mark. In the mid-range between 20% and 30%, countries such as Latvia, Malta, Austria, Thailand, and Jamaica are visible. A cluster of European nations, including Estonia and Australia, sits slightly below the 20% line. At the lower end of the vertical scale, Kazakhstan is positioned near 0%, while Bermuda, Saint Kitts and Nevis, and Curacao fall between 5% and 10%. On the horizontal axis, countries like Burundi, Niger, and DR Congo are located at the far left, indicating GDP per capita values near or below $2,000. Conversely, nations such as Hong Kong, Andorra, and Bermuda extend toward the right side of the chart, approaching or exceeding $50,000. The distribution shows no single linear trend, with high expenditure shares appearing across both low and high GDP per capita levels.
